events.title: WHO and Brazil urge world leaders to finalise Pandemic Agreement to prevent future global health crises
events.description: Global officials are calling on world leaders to finalise a crucial international agreement aimed at preventing future pandemics, according to a joint letter issued on Monday.

events.title: Colombian military neutralizes five terrorists accused in bus bombing that killed 20, injured 45 civilians
events.description: Colombian military forces neutralized five members of the militant group allegedly responsible for the bus bombing that killed 20 civilians in Cauca.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Colombian military neutralizes five terrorists accused in bus bombing that killed 20, injured 45 civilians

events.title: Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk
events.description: Abdiqadir Salah was pierced by shrapnel in a bombing that killed 12 in Somalia. But as the US denies civilians were hurt they face no hope of compensation Read more: Killed walking home from school: why did Somali children become targets of US drone strikes? A seven-year-old boy who was riddled with shrapnel during a deadly US airstrike in Somalia faces losing his ability to walk unless he has a £750 emergency operation. But Abdiqadir Salah’s family cannot afford the surgery and the US – which refuses to admit that any civilians were killed or injured during its attack six months ago – appears unwilling to pay compensation to those affected by airstrikes in Somalia. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk

events.title: Misleading images turn Iran’s World Cup debut into a political flashpoint
events.description: As Iran began its World Cup campaign amidst a tense political climate, a wave of misleading images and claims spread online about the team making political statements. One photo falsely claimed to show an Iranian player carrying a pink school backpack in tribute to Iranian schoolgirls killed in Minab during the US-Iran conflict. A goal celebration by Iranian forward Mohamed Mohebi was also widely interpreted online as a "threatening" gun gesture.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Misleading images turn Iran’s World Cup debut into a political flashpoint

events.title: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
events.description: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Geneva on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts

events.title: Ukraine: Latest Russian attack kills civilians, damages cultural landmark
events.description: Several civilians were killed and dozens more were injured in the latest wave of overnight attacks in Ukraine that targeted the capital Kyiv, the city of Kharkiv and the country’s history and cultural heritage, the United Nations said on Monday.

events.title: MSF sacks 18 staff over sexual exploitation and abuse of Sudanese refugees in Chad
events.description: Dozens of Doctors Without Borders staff have been accused of sexually abusing Sudanese refugees in Chad, the organisation said Monday, adding that it dismissed 18 workers after investigations found "serious misconduct". The medical charity, which goes by its French acronym MSF, said it had launched a series of investigations into "serious allegations of sexual exploitation and abuse" put forward in late 2024 by Sudanese refugees hosted in eastern Chad. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Charles Bouessel, Senior Analyst for Central Africa at the International Crisis Group.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MSF sacks 18 staff over sexual exploitation and abuse of Sudanese refugees in Chad

events.title: UNHCR campaign to defend fundamental human right to asylum ahead of World Refugee Day
events.description: The UNHCR launched it's "Until Everyone is Safe" campaign. It's a call to young people across the world to reclaim and defend the fundamental human right of asylum as a shared global good. •The campaign challenges stereotypes against refugees, emphasizing that the right to seek safety is a crucial lifeline that means far more than the absence of war and violence. It encompasses the legal right to work, access health care, pursue an education, and live with dignity—the exact tools needed to move beyond survival and rebuild a future. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Eujin Byun, Global Spokesperson for the UNHCR, the UN refugee agency.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UNHCR campaign to defend fundamental human right to asylum ahead of World Refugee Day

events.title: Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children
events.description: Drought, extreme heat and heatwaves are the most prevalent trio of hazards endangering millions of children globally, warned a newly released climate report by the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children

events.title: Why are experts warning latest Ebola outbreak could be ‘worst ever’?
events.description: The virus is spreading faster than health workers can track it and international funding has slumped.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why are experts warning latest Ebola outbreak could be ‘worst ever’?

events.title: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
events.description: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

events.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds
events.description: Canadian officials find structural defects in material used for hull and say firm failed to fully test ‘novel’ design Canadian safety officials have issued a damning report on the catastrophic final voyage of the Titan submersible, finding that the US company behind the expedition was overcome by “groupthink” and “confirmation bias” and failed to understand the profound risks confronting their largely untested craft. The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the Atlantic Ocean in June 2023 en route to the wreckage of the Titanic ocean liner. But nearly two hours after it departed with five passengers, communications went dark. The disappearance prompted a frantic international search, with Canada and the US marshalling all available resources . Continue reading...

events.title: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
events.description: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation

events.title: Trump says 'nobody' attacked Iranian girls' school 'on purpose'
events.description: Donald Trump has said "nobody" purposefully attacked an Iranian girls' school where scores of people were killed in a strike on the first day of the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump says 'nobody' attacked Iranian girls' school 'on purpose'

events.title: Sudan’s young women return to international soccer as war and taboos linger
events.description: Sudan's under-17 women's national soccer team has made its first international appearance since civil war erupted in the country

events.title: DC Guard shooting suspect stares down death penalty in first court appearance

events.title: Worsening hunger could push millions closer to famine in 13 global hotspots
events.description: Millions of people across some of the world’s most vulnerable regions are expected to face worsening hunger in the months ahead, as conflict, economic pressures and shrinking aid budgets exacerbate long-running crises.

events.title: What we know about Russian warship that fired warning shots in English Channel
events.description: A Russian warship fired warning shots at a UK-registered yacht in the English Channel in an incident that has intensified national security concerns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What we know about Russian warship that fired warning shots in English Channel

events.title: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
events.description: Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on women, peace and security
events.description: The UN Security Council has been meeting in New York for its annual open debate on women, peace and security, in line with a 25-year-old resolution on the issue. The Secretary-General’s most recent report shows that in 2024, women made up only seven percent of negotiators on average worldwide, and nearly nine out of 10 negotiation tracks included no women negotiators at all. Follow live coverage below, and for daily coverage, check out our full meetings coverage here.

events.title: UN urges adherence to mine ban treaty
events.description: Countries must uphold international law limiting the use of anti-personnel mines, which kill and maim civilians long after conflicts have ended, the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said in a report published on Tuesday.

events.title: Two children dead after pram hit by car in Sydney despite ‘phenomenal’ bystander efforts to save them
events.description: Police found two children – both aged under five – had been struck by a car in Cabramatta, in the city’s south-west Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two children in a pram have been struck and killed by a car in Sydney’s south-west. Emergency services were called to the intersection of Joseph and Gilbert streets, Cabramatta at about 3.15pm on Wednesday after reports of a crash, New South Wales police said. Continue reading...

events.title: Diluting uranium, oil sales: What's in the draft US-Iran deal?
events.description: Senior US officials on Wednesday disclosed the contents of a draft memorandum of understanding with Iran, ending days of secrecy ahead of a planned signing ceremony on Friday. The document outlines an immediate ceasefire, steps toward lifting US sanctions, the restoration of commercial shipping and negotiations on Iran’s nuclear programme.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Diluting uranium, oil sales: What's in the draft US-Iran deal?

events.title: G7 summit hailed a success by Macron thanks to developments on Ukraine and Iran
events.description: Ukraine has won key pledges of further support from world leaders in defending itself from Russia's full-scale invasion, now in its fifth year. Leaders attending the summit promised to strengthen Ukraine’s air defenses and ensure its energy supply, as well as step up international economic pressure on Moscow. This comes as US President Donald Trump, bolstered by the memorandum of understanding with Iran, shifted away from Moscow. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us what to know.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 G7 summit hailed a success by Macron thanks to developments on Ukraine and Iran

events.title: Suspect in custody after Delaware hospital shooting leaves at least 1 person dead, another wounded
events.description: Two people were shot at Wilmington Hospital in Delaware on Tuesday that left one of the victims dead. A suspect has been taken into custody.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Suspect in custody after Delaware hospital shooting leaves at least 1 person dead, another wounded

events.title: Morocco: The 2030 World Cup is more than sports, it is a national project
events.description: Hosting the 2030 World Cup is more than just a sporting event for Rabat. While the kingdom invests heavily in infrastructure and prestige projects, doubts and social conflicts grow alongside a sense of pride and hope.

events.title: AFP investigates banner stunt during Pauline Hanson’s National Press Club speech

In a statement on Wednesday afternoon, the National Press Club said it had referred the incident to police after concluding none of its own staff were involved. Continue reading...

events.title: South Africa commemorates 50 year-old anniversary of Soweto uprising
events.description: South Africa on June 16 marked the 50th anniversary of the Soweto uprising, when over 200 young people protesting against the apartheid education system were shot and killed by the police. The event is considered a turning point in South Africa's liberation struggle against white minority rule. But for many, the day downplays the violence the protesters were met with.

events.title: As crime surges in some Latin American countries, a far-right backlash is brewing

events.title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel

events.title: Toronto police link dozens of shootings to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network
events.description: Young adults and teens are being recruited through apps like Telegram and paid to carry out attacks, officials say Police investigators in Toronto have said that dozens of shootings – including one at the US consulate in March – are linked to a “multilayered” gun-for-hire network that is also responsible for attacks on synagogues around Canada’s largest city. Toronto’s police chief, Myron Demkiw, told reporters on Tuesday that young adults and teenagers are being recruited through encrypted messaging apps such as Signal, Telegram and WhatsApp by “bad actors” and paid by the networks to carry out the attacks. Shooters are required to film their attacks in order to get paid. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Toronto police link dozens of shootings to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network

events.description: The One Nation leader offered a series of inflammatory statements at the National Press Club that may play well with her supporters but will alarm many others Pauline Hanson says Australia ‘must be monocultural’ in National Press Club speech Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast For months, voters have been telling anyone who’ll listen that they want Pauline Hanson. events.title: Protesters to rally against World Cup sponsor Hyundai before Mexico game
events.description: Focus on business dealings with mining company Guadalajara rally to highlight fate of ‘disappeared’ Hyundai will be targeted by protesters at a rally before the Group A game between Mexico and South Korea in Guadalajara on Thursday, due to the World Cup sponsor’s business dealings with the South American mining company Ternium. A 2025 report from the environmental group Mighty Earth criticised Hyundai’s involvement in what they described as a “dirty steel supply chain”, as the South Korean motor company is a major buyer of iron ore from Ternium for use in steel production. Ternium has faced repeated criticisms for its destructive environmental impact and corporate governance policies from campaign groups, as well as its alleged links to the disappearance of two Mexican activists. Continue reading...

events.title: UN chief visits Haiti, where a new 'gang-suppression force' will be deployed
events.description: U.N. Secretary-General António Guterres's visit to Port-au-Prince comes as gang violence persists. According to U.N. data, 2,300 people have been killed in Haiti this year, with another 100 kidnapped.

events.title: Global fish production hits new high, but climate threats loom
events.description: Aquaculture now supplies most of the fish people eat, a landmark report by the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) reveals, but rising temperatures and overfishing are putting the future of the industry at risk.

events.title: Bank of Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high … of 1%
events.description: Country acts amid Iran war inflation pressures, but US Fed and Bank of England expected to hold rates Business live – latest updates The Bank of Japan (BoJ) has raised interest rates to a 31-year high as it tries to dampen inflationary pressures created by the Iran war. Policymakers in Tokyo raised the BoJ’s short-term policy rate by a quarter of one percentage point, to 1% from 0.75% , and warned that companies were passing on rising oil costs to each other at a “relatively fast pace”. Continue reading...

events.title: Monica Juma: Steering global cooperation at a time of converging threats

events.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring

More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. events.title: Ghana to advance reparatory justice at first major gathering since landmark UN resolution
events.description: Heads of state and participants from more than 80 countries at three-day event in Accra to pursue actionable commitments to reconciliation and restitution Ghana is hosting a conference to advance the continent’s push for reparatory justice after the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Heads of state and government, ministers, civil society representatives, historians, researchers and legal experts representing more than 80 countries are converging in the capital, Accra, for the three-day event, billed Next Steps , which starts on Wednesday. It is the first major gathering on the issue since the resolution was adopted . Continue reading...

events.title: Germany: Reports of antisemitism in 2025 remain high
events.description: The Federal Association of Departments for Research and Information on Antisemitism (RIAS) recorded over 8,700 cases they classify as antisemitic hostility in 2025, many of which were connected with the State of Israel.

events.title: From camel coats to guochao: Max Mara woos China’s luxury brand consumers

events.title: World Cup 2026: Resilient DR Congo hold off Ronaldo's Portugal onslaught
events.description: Portugal and DR Congo played out a dramatic 1-1 draw in Houston as the African underdogs showed remarkable resilience against one of the tournament's favourites. João Neves gave the Portuguese an early lead, but Yoane Wissa's equaliser just before half-time earned the Leopards a crucial point in their first World Cup appearance since 1974.

events.title: G7 focuses on AI's contentious future and US industry dominance
events.description: G7 leaders were joined by some of the world's top tech executives on June 17 to discuss opportunities and dangers of artificial intelligence. The future of the industry is highly contentious - with US businesses dominating the industries and many fearful that the powerful new technology poses substantial risks. For G7 leaders, AI poses many questions – such as how to ensure the technology respects human dignity and can be kept under control.

events.title: The US-Iran agreement: Breakthrough or bluff?
events.description: 👉Listen to The World with Richard Engel and Yalda Hakim on your podcast app👈

events.title: 15 countries in Kenya adopt the Mombasa declaration to fight illegal fishing
events.description: Fifteen countries have adopted the Mombasa declaration to combat illegal fishing, which experts say costs the global economy up to $50 billion annually

events.title: Migrants clash with police at a deportation site in South Africa where thousands have gathered
events.description: Police have fired rubber bullets and used stun grenades against migrants protesting near a community hall in South Africa where thousands are being processed for deportation

events.title: Zelenskyy says G7 leaders pledge more vital help for Ukraine against Russia

events.title: Kenya accused of deporting Taiwanese conference delegates on China's behalf

events.title: Anti-G7 protest turns violent as demonstrators torch Tesla and smash UN office windows

events.title: Middle East: Trump could restart Iran war if deal not signed

events.title: West Bank: Israeli settlers torch mosque
events.description: Israeli settlers set fire to a mosque in a West Bank village on Wednesday, local officials and witnesses said. Videos on social media showed the mosque in the town of Jiljilya ablaze while walls were marked with Hebrew graffiti reading “revenge”.

events.title: Body found in search for Briton missing off Portugal coast
events.description: A body has been found after a British man went missing while swimming in the sea in Portugal last week.

events.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
events.description: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

events.title: A turning point for Haiti? New security force takes fight to powerful gangs
events.description: Passengers look out of the back of a brightly-coloured shared pick-up style taxi, known locally in Haiti as a “tap tap” as it makes its way along the Boulevard du 15 Octobre in the east of the capital, Port-au-Prince.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Pauline Hanson’s speech ‘shameful’ and echoed ‘rubbish’ from rightwing figures in UK and US, critics say
events.description: Labor minister says Australians would lose their jobs and wages would go down if One Nation’s policies became law Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson has been roundly condemned for her National Press Club speech, with advocacy groups accusing the One Nation leader of using “hatred for political gain” while the Greens say Hanson is echoing “rubbish” lines from rightwing figures in the UK and US. In Hanson’s first address after 20 years in politics, the leader claimed transgender ideology “has penetrated almost every regulatory authority”. Continue reading...

events.title: Taiwan says Chinese pressure over the island is the 'new normal'
events.description: Taiwan's Foreign Ministry on Tuesday said the scholars' passports and mobile phones were confiscated, and they were detained in Mombasa for more than 20 hours before being allowed to leave the country.

events.title: US, allies to make plan for $300 billion Iran reconstruction fund
events.description: Iran could get access to a $300 billion reconstruction fund under a deal with the United States, US officials said Wednesday as they released the text, while insisting that Washington was under no commitment to contribute. Tehran would also be allowed to resume oil sales as soon as the agreement is signed this week, while all sanctions would be lifted if a final deal is reached after a 60-day negotiation period. FRANCE 24's Caitlin Brown reports.

events.title: France seal hard-earned win against Senegal, Messi scores hat-trick for Argentina
events.description: Tactical changes by Les Bleus manager Didier Deschamps at half-time saw France seal a 3-1 win in their opening game against Senegal. Kylian Mbappé scored a brace, becoming the country's top goalscorer, with 58 goals to his name. Meanwhile, Lionel Messi helped Argentina shine with a hat-trick against Algeria – enough to put pressure on Cristiano Ronaldo, whose Portuguese side will face DR Congo this Wednesday?

events.title: Alleged Charlie Kirk assassin Tyler Robinson's roommate and lover received limited immunity: prosecutors
events.description: Lance Twiggs, transgender roommate and partner of Charlie Kirk's alleged assassin Tyler Robinson, was given limited immunity for a recorded statement.

events.title: Elon Musk demands prison for politicians who 'turned a blind eye' to grooming gangs as new report released

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: EU lawmakers approve new tougher migrant policy
events.description: The EU is backing tougher migration policies that seek to ramp up deportations of failed asylum-seekers. Under the new legislation, EU states can establish "return hubs."

events.title: South Korea to shrink security buffer zone to North Korea
events.description: South Korea will ease restrictions on roughly 270 square kilometers of land near the border with North Korea. Farmers in the area had long complained of rules that complicated working conditions.

events.title: Germany news: Ministers discuss refugee deportation rules
events.description: Germany's regional interior ministers are holding their biannual conference with immigration, deportations and benefits high on the agenda. Meanwhile, Germany and Poland are marking 35 years of cooperation. DW has more.

events.title: Not all skinny: The French do get fat
events.description: With the French healthcare system now reimbursing the anti-obesity drugs Wegovy and Mounjaro, we explain what the strict conditions are to benefit from them. We discuss France's rates of obesity and the areas where they're highest. Finally, we discuss the common stereotype that French people don't get fat. While not true, it does contain a sliver of truth when comparing France to other EU nations.

events.title: Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump’s Iran pressure campaign after regional trip: ‘Zero daylight’
events.description: Ambassador Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump's blockade and economic pressure campaign against Iran, with UAE and Bahrain validating U.S. strategy.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand

events.title: Sierra Leone’s first lady refuses to condemn FGM without ‘reliable data’ on harms
events.description: Exclusive: health professionals, survivors and politicians voice concerns in open letter over comments by Fatima Maada Bio, who denies supporting the practice The first lady of Sierra Leone has denied that she supports female genital mutilation amid rising anger around her perceived approval of the practice. But in an exclusive response to the Guardian, Fatima Maada Bio, the wife of President Julius Maada Bio, also said she would not openly condemn FGM until she saw “reliable data” that the practice was harmful. Continue reading...

events.title: Recently single Australian men are seven times more likely to report a suicide attempt, study shows
events.description: Fathers particularly affected, with almost 15% reporting suicidal plans after relationship breakdowns, according to survey of 20,000 men Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Men who have recently gone through a breakup are seven times more likely to report a suicide attempt than those who haven’t, a new study shows The Ten to Men project has followed more than 20,000 Australian men and identified the mental health risks in relationship breakdowns, which can include changes in their contact with their children, in their finances, and in their support network. Continue reading...

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Lives and incomes lost as Ebola takes toll on Bunia’s public-facing workers
events.description: A headteacher, a motorcycle taxi driver and a travel agent are among those who are counting the human and economic cost of the virus Justin Keno watches more than 400 pupils stream through the Nelson Mandela school’s gate each morning, and wonders which of them might be carrying Ebola. The institution’s principal has done everything he can to prevent the spread of the virus: installing hand-washing basins at the entrance, providing alcohol-based hand rub for parents, making pupils bring packed lunches instead of eating in the canteen, and banning food sellers from outside the gates. Continue reading...

events.title: Jair Bolsonaro’s son sentenced to four years in jail for seeking US interference in father’s Brazil coup trial
events.description: Brazil supreme court finds that Eduardo Bolsonaro – who resides in the US - tried to get sanctions put on judges trying ex-president over coup plot Brazil’s supreme court has sentenced Eduardo Bolsonaro to four years and two months in prison after finding him guilty of courting US ⁠interference in his father’s coup plot trial last year. The office of Brazil’s ‌prosecutor general had ‌charged Eduardo Bolsonaro – who lives in the US - courting interference from the Trump administration to help Jair Bolsonaro’s case, by imposing sanctions on the court’s justices and tariffs on Brazilian goods. Continue reading...

events.title: Canada eliminates human rights watchdog that oversees companies operating abroad

The move comes as Canada faces criticism from Donald Trump ’s administration over its “unacceptable” efforts to combat forced labour. Continue reading...

events.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
events.description: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. events.description: NATO's secretary general went to great lengths to cast a positive light on the alliance ahead of a ministers' meeting. The closest he came to addressing any tension was saying members have "a lot to talk about."

events.title: Why the Williams sisters and other stars are playing longer
events.description: More than three decades after they burst onto the tennis scene, Venus and Serena Williams are still playing at the top level. Why are we seeing elite athletes carrying on into their 40s and beyond?

events.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
events.description: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

events.title: Replay: Macron holds press conference as G7 summit comes to an end
events.description: Rewatch French President Emmanuel Macron's press conference as the G7 summit comes to a close.

events.title: France examines bill to rein in ultra-fast-fashion's expansion
events.description: Here in Paris, Chinese fast-fashion brand Shein is leaving the iconic BHV department store in the chic Marais neighbourhood. The departure follows months of controversy over its inclusion at the landmark shop - with critics accusing Shein's low-cost, high turnover business model of destroying French retail, and high-end brands like Dior and Guerlain abandoning BHV. A French parliamentary committee begins on June 17 an examination of a proposed law to rein in the expansion of ultra-fast fashion.

events.title: Replay: Trump meets Modi, addresses memorandum of understanding in Iran and FRANCE 24's analysis
events.description: Rewatch as US President Donald Trump meets Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, and addresses memorandum of understanding with Iran.

events.title: From YouTube to box office history: The Gen Z director rewriting the rules of cinema
events.description: On this week's film show, critic Emma Jones joins Eve Jackson to review the latest cinema releases. They discuss "Backrooms", the record-breaking horror hit from 20-year-old director Kane Parsons, now the youngest filmmaker ever to top the US box office. Starring Chiwetel Ejiofor and Renate Reinsve, the film is shaking up Hollywood's ideas about who gets to make movies.

events.title: Killing of Russian artist who criticised Putin 'has hallmarks of political murder'
events.description: The killing of a Russian artist who was critical of Vladimir Putin has the hallmarks of a "political murder", according to Poland's prime minister.

events.title: Why Ukraine could now have fresh hope of vital US support
events.description: The official G7 agenda started on Tuesday morning with a two-hour session on Ukraine.

events.title: Uganda court charges lawyer for jailed Ugandan opposition leader with concealing treason
events.description: A court in Uganda has charged Erias Lukwago, an attorney for jailed opposition leader Kizza Besigye, with concealing treason

events.title: Japanese fair trade officials raid 6 ice cream makers on suspicion of price fixing
events.description: Six major Japanese ice cream makers have been raided by the government fair trade watchdog officials on suspicion of price fixing

events.title: Killing of Russian artist in Poland has hallmarks of political assassination, prime minister says
events.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk says the killing of a Russian artist critical of President Vladimir Putin looks like a political assassination

events.title: Microhistory pioneer Carlo Ginzburg, who gave voice to the marginalized, dies at 87
events.description: Carlo Ginzburg, a renowned Italian historian, has died at 87
events.ticker_label: 💥 Microhistory pioneer Carlo Ginzburg, who gave voice to the marginalized, dies at 87

events.title: Philippine Senate president allied with Duterte removed ahead of his daughter's impeachment trial
events.description: A leadership standoff in the Philippine Senate has ended with the removal of an ally of former President Rodrigo Duterte as Senate leader
events.ticker_label: 💥 Philippine Senate president allied with Duterte removed ahead of his daughter's impeachment trial

events.title: Spain's former PM Zapatero faces questioning by judge in corruption probe
events.description: Former Spanish Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero has appeared before a judge in Madrid

events.title: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit has received a lung transplant
events.description: Norway's royal house says Crown Princess Mette-Marit has undergone a successful lung transplant

events.title: Africa's clean energy projects face financing barrier from credit rating rules
events.description: Billions pledged for Africa’s clean energy transition are failing to translate into projects on the ground as high financing costs deter investors

events.title: Jeanine Pirro calls out 'dirtbags' after 14 charged in alleged cocaine ring operating feet from a schoolyard
events.description: Fourteen suspects charged in a cocaine trafficking scheme near a D.C. elementary school, where dealers allegedly sold drugs in front of children.

events.title: Judge reveals Luigi Mangione will pursue psychiatric defense in UnitedHealthcare CEO assassination case
events.description: Luigi Mangione's defense team plans to argue he suffered extreme emotional disturbance during the alleged UnitedHealthcare CEO murder, judge says.

events.title: Trump wins two, loses one as billionaire delivers rare blow to endorsement machine and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Karmelo Anthony claims he's penniless as business records tied to parents draw scrutiny
events.description: Convicted killer Karmelo Anthony claims he is penniless while seeking a taxpayer-funded attorney, but records show his parents formed a Texas business.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Karmelo Anthony claims he's penniless as business records tied to parents draw scrutiny

events.title: Israel launches 'Biblical Highway' amid campaign to erase Jewish history
events.description: Israel's government brands Route 60 as the Biblical Highway, aiming to strengthen heritage tourism and the nation's identity as the land of the Bible.

events.title: 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope
events.description: A 21-year-old woman died after instructors launched her from a bridge without attaching safety ropes during a rope-jumping event near São Paulo, Brazil.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 Brazilian men charged after woman tossed from bridge without safety rope

events.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
events.description: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.

events.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
events.description: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
events.description: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.
